Named Locations

Contents

  • 1 Naming
  • 2 Named Locations
    • 2.1 Fortnite: Battle Royale
    • 2.2 Fortnite OG
    • 2.3 Fortnite: Reload
    • 2.4 Blitz Royale
    • 2.5 Party Royale
  • 3 Trivia

Current Map

  • Battle Royale
  • OG

Overview

Named Locations, commonly known as Points of Interest (abbreviated as POI), are the largest types of locations in Fortnite. They are named on the map, often using alliteration as their naming convention. However, this is not always the case. Named Locations were introduced on launch of the game, in the Pre-Season.

They are mostly large in size - often resembling towns and cities, and because of this, many Chests and Ammo Boxes can be found in these locations.

Naming

Naming[]

Named Locations often follow an alliterative ruleset, meaning the location is two words, each of which start with the same letter, the first word being usually an adjective. Examples are:

  • Greasy Grove
  • Coney Crossroads
  • Frenzy Farm
  • Creeky Compound
  • Shattered Slabs
  • Latte Landing

However, over the seasons and chapters, more and more POIs have stopped following this ruleset for various reasons:

  • Many faction specific locations, such as The Citadel, or The Joneses do not use alliteration. On demotion, some of these have gained alliterative status, such as The Rig becoming Rickety Rig, and some lost it, such as Classified Canyon becoming New Sanctuary
  • Collaboration Locations often do not use alliteration, such as The Daily Bugle or Gotham City.
    • This is not always the case, as Doom's Domain was alliterative, despite being collaboration based.
  • Some locations are single words, such as Sanctuary, BURD, and Wonkeeland, and so can't be alliterative.
  • Some locations cannot be confirmed as to why they do not use alliteration, such as The Pyramid or The Spire.
  • Some locations do not follow any Naming Theme, such as Moisty Palms, Chromejam Junction, Reality Falls, Chonker's Speedway or Anvil Square.
  • Some locations that use alliteration, don't start with the same letter, but use the same phonetic sound, such as Knotty Nets, Kenjutsu Crossing or Seaport City.
  • In the rare instance, some locations also use a ruleset of rhyming, such as Rave Cave
Named Locations

Named Locations[]

Fortnite: Battle Royale[]

  • Golden Coast
  • Springfield Island
  • Oninoshima
  • Helios
  • Asteria
  • Artemis
  • Apollo
  • Athena

Fortnite OG[]

  • Athena

This is the island from Fortnite OG Chapter 1.

Unlike Fortnite: Battle Royale Chapter 1, The Block missed its landing and became an Unnamed Location, leaving Risky Reels for the remainder of the Season 7 in its place. This is the only difference for Named Locations.

Replaced by Brutal Bulwark in Update v37.50 (Week 17: Brutal Bulwark).

Anvil Park Added alongside the Asteria Awakened island in Update v37.10 (Week 11: Asteria Awakened).
Slap Factory Replaced Sandy Sheets in Update v37.31 (Week 15: Slap Factory).
Brutal Bulwark Replaced Snobby Shoals in Update v37.50 (Week 17: Brutal Bulwark).
The Prison Added in Update v37.51 (Week 20: The Walking Dead).
Removed in Update v38.10 (Week 22: Mortal Kombat).

Name Image Note

Returned alongside the Papaya island in Update v30.20.

Trivia

Trivia[]

  • Many Locations have shared names, been demoted, or been brought back as demoted locations. The following trivia denotes these:
    • Stealthy Stronghold was changed to an Unnamed Location after Chapter 2: Season 6 concluded. From its inclusion in Chapter 2: Season 5, it was a named location.
    • Loot Lake was a Landmark on the Artemis Island, despite being a named POI in Chapter 1 (Its appearance on Artemis is significantly different).
    • Flush Factory was brought back as a Landmark named Flushed Building.
    • Part of Greasy Grove was brought back as a Landmark named The Durrr Burger.
    • Parts of Tomato Town were brought back as a Landmark named The Pizza Pit.
    • Dusty Depot has been brought back multiple times as a Landmark, named Dusted Depot and Displaced Depot respectively.
    • The Grotto was flooded, becoming a Landmark named Brutus' Basin.
    • The Shark was flooded and destroyed becoming a Landmark named Sharky Shell.
    • Craggy Cliffs was supposed to be named Beachy Bluffs which became a Landmark in Chapter 4: Season 1.
  • Chapter 6: Season 4 holds the record for most new Named Locations in a season (O.X.R. HQ, The Hive, Ranger's Ruin, Swarmy Stash, Demon's Debris, Dystopia City, Bugswatter Beach, Dreamy Domain, Harmony Hills, Ranger's Rest, Creepy Camps, Freaky Fields, Gourdy Gate, Pumpkin Pass, and Viney Shafts).
    • The record is 15. The previous was Chapter 3: Season 4 with 12.
  • Chapter 1: Season 3 holds the record for the least amount of new Named Locations in a season, with only Lucky Landing being introduced.
  • The Pyramid and The Convergence are the only named locations that do not have any destructible objects, aside from trees and rocks which fall inside the named location.
    • Tilted Town does not let you build or break inside of the named location.
  • The Daily Bugle is the first Location to have three words in its name.
    • In other languages, some named location having been translated also have three words, this is for example the case of Doom's Domain and Reality Falls which have been named "Fief de Fatalis" and "Cascades de Réalité" in French.
    • Kappa Kappa Factory is the second location to have three words in its name.
    • Springfield Town Square and Cletus' Corn Hole are the third and fourth locations to have three words in its name.
  • Sanctuary is the first Location to have one word in its name, the second being Nitrodrome, the third being Doomstadt, the fourth being Burd, and the fifth being Wonkeeland.
    • In other languages, some named location having been translated also have one word, this is for example the case of Gotham City and The Block which have been named "Gotham" and "Podium" in Polish.
  • Springfield Nuclear Power Plant is the first location to have four words in its name.
    • It also currently has the longest name out of any named location in Battle Royale history, being 28 characters long (31 with spaces).
      • The previous record holder was Brawler's Battleground, which has 21 characters. (22 with spaces)
  • Logjam Lotus is one of the only named locations to drastically change appearance every match. Logjam Lotus has a variety of different themes, including an Atlantis theme, pirate theme, roman theme, and a wild west theme. These themes take heavy inspiration from other named locations such as Lazy Lagoon, Colossal Coliseum, and Tilted Town respectively.
  • Chapter 6: Season 1 is the starting season of a Chapter with the most Named Locations ever, starting the chapter with 18 Named Locations.
    • In fact, that season holds the record for the highest amount of Named Locations in a season.
    • Chapter 1 started with 10 Named Locations.
    • Chapter 2 started with 13 Named Locations.
    • Chapter 3 started with 12 Named Locations but gained 2 others after during the season.
    • Chapter 4 started with 9 Named Locations.
    • Chapter 5 started with 11 Named Locations.
    • Chapter 7 started with 13 Named Locations.
